title: Set correct line info for enums and structs
body:
Both `netenum_t` and and `netstruct_t` inherit from `LineInfo`. This information is used when generating error messages relating to these types. But the line info is not always set correctly, which can result in confusing error messages. E.g.

```
:0: error: Member r of packed struct/union must be packed.
```

For structs the line info is never set.

For enums the line info is set to the location where a signal of that type is declared. This works OK when it is a direction enum signal declaration since the declaration location of the signal and the type are the same. But if the enum is declared inside an aggregate type such as a class or struct it is never set. If the enum is a typedef it will be set to the location of the last signal declaration using that type.

Set the LineInfo for both of them so that it always points to the location of the declaration of the data type.
code_blocks: :0: error: Member r of packed struct/union must be packed.
